paper_id: Calhau et al. - 2024 - A System Core Ontology for Capability Emergence Modeling
input_limitations: Text extraction includes encoding artifacts in accented characters and punctuation, and figures are represented only as [FIGURE].

models.model_label: System core ontology for capability emergence modeling

evidence_quotes.quote: In pursuit of an understanding of the emergence phenomenon and the nature of those new kinds of systems in organizations, we propose a well-founded system core ontology based on the Unified Foundational Ontology.
To address this issue, this work aimed at proposing a well-founded system ontology based on Unified Foundational Ontology [20].

goals.justification: The paper explicitly states that the ontology is proposed in pursuit of understanding emergence and systems in organizations, matching the domain understanding goal.

evidence_quotes.quote: The use of the system ontology to create language patterns in EA notations will be detailed in the following case study.
To show the benefits of the system ontology and the guidelines shown above, we have applied them to a real-life case study from the literature to improve the capability emergence modeling.

This system ontology can also be used as a reference to integrate ontologies of distinct kinds of systems such as cyber-physical systems, systemof-systems, and digital twins, besides contributing to interoperability and data integration in different knowledge areas.

goals.justification: The ontology is explicitly used or intended as a basis for creating language patterns and integrating ontologies, which fits the rule for implementation, integration, or artifact generation.

evidence_quotes.quote: The ontology aims to: (i) improve the capability emergence modeling (in EA notations) of socio-technical systems into enterprises and; (ii) facilitate the identification of capability emergence patterns by using some pattern recognition technique.

In this case, for example, the better team's structure (roles and relationships) and professional characteristics (desired capabilities for each role) can be identified from data of the more performative teams (with better KPIs) of an organization.

goals.justification: The ontology is framed as supporting identification of emergence patterns and better organizational structures; this implies analysis for deciding what should change, although the paper does not foreground decision-making language.

evidence_quotes.quote: In this case, the identified patterns can even be considered as a knowledge items into the knowledge repository of the organization and forming a 'library' of patterns.

uncertain_goals.reason: The repository evidence concerns identified emergence patterns rather than clearly stating that the ontology itself is used as asynchronous documentation.
